goals: Allow developers to generate music from a text prompt with a single function
All tests pass on CPU (mocked weights).

userStories.title: Add `vae_decode_audio` primitive
userStories.description: As a developer using comfy-diffusion, I want a `vae_decode_audio(vae, latent)` function in `comfy_diffusion/audio.py` so that I can decode ACE Step (and any compatible audio) latents into a waveform dict without calling ComfyUI node internals directly.

userStories.title: Checkpoint pipeline (`ace_step_1_5_t2a_checkpoint`)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `comfy_diffusion.pipelines.audio.ace_step.v1_5.checkpoint.run(...)` so that I can generate audio using the single all-in-one checkpoint (`ace_step_1.5_turbo_aio.safetensors`) without managing separate model files.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Node execution order mirrors the workflow exactly:
Default sampler parameters match the workflow: `steps=8`, `cfg=1.0`,

userStories.title: Split pipeline — 1.7B text encoder (`ace_step_1_5_t2a_split`)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `comfy_diffusion.pipelines.audio.ace_step.v1_5.split.run(...)` so that I can use the memory-efficient split variant with separate UNet, 0.6B + 1.7B text encoders, and VAE.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Sampling execution follows the same order as US-002 (steps 2–7).

userStories.title: Split pipeline — 4B text encoder (`ace_step_1_5_t2a_split_4b`)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `comfy_diffusion.pipelines.audio.ace_step.v1_5.split_4b.run(...)` so that I can use the higher-capacity 4B text encoder variant for richer conditioning.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Sampling execution follows the same order as US-002 (steps 2–7).

userStories.title: Package scaffolding for audio pipelines
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the `comfy_diffusion.pipelines.audio` sub-package to exist and be importable so that imports like `from comfy_diffusion.pipelines.audio.ace_step.v1_5.checkpoint import run` work out of the box.

userStories.title: CPU-passing tests for all three pipelines
userStories.description: As a developer, I want pytest tests that validate the pipeline structure (manifest shape, run signature, return type) on CPU without loading real model weights so that CI passes with no GPU.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Tests verify `manifest()` returns a non-empty list of `ModelEntry` items
Tests verify `manifest()` model filenames and destination paths match the
Tests for `run()` mock model loading and sampling; assert return dict
`uv run pytest tests/test_ace_step_v1_5_pipelines.py` passes on CPU.

functionalRequirements.description: ** All three pipeline modules export exactly `manifest()` and `run()`
** `manifest()` in each pipeline module is the single source of truth
** Default sampler settings match the workflows: `steps=8`, `cfg=1.0`,
** `ModelSamplingAuraFlow` shift is `3` for all ACE Step 1.5 pipelines
** All imports of `comfy.*` and `torch` inside pipeline `run()`
** No `torch` or `comfy.*` imports at the module top level in any new
